title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Commissioner Delmon and Sir Henry Clithering discuss a letter received from an "old maid" staying at the Meddenham Royal Spa Hotel, which contains information regarding the Chipping Cleghorn case. The writer is identified as Jane Mapple. Sir Henry suggests that Mapple is a highly observant individual who has traveled to Meddingham to assist with the murder investigation.

The investigators meet Miss Mapple, a kind, elderly woman who is the aunt of the author Raymond West. She joins the group in the private office of Mr. Rolleston to present her findings. Miss Mapple reveals that a young man working at the hotel counter—who was previously believed to have directed a robbery and shot himself—actually forged a check. She produces the check, showing how he altered a seven-pound amount to seventeen pounds using a smudge of ink. Miss Mapple explains that she noticed the discrepancy because of her thrifty nature. She compares his deceptive gaze to a local fishmonger, Fred Taylor, and suggests the young man is a professional.

Miss Mapple further observes that Rudy Schlesinger, who has a criminal record in Switzerland, is likely using a fake ID. She notes that Schlesinger frequently spends money on a red-haired waitress and suspects the waitress is withholding information because she is afraid to testify. Finally, Miss Mapple posits that the young man did not act alone. She argues that while he might commit small thefts to fund a social life, his violent escalation into a hostage situation involving a revolver is inconsistent with his character, suggesting someone else must have coerced him. This observation resonates strongly with Inspector Corcoran.
